summary: Tested OpenAI API key settings integration and table assignment with AI clash detection. All 8 backend tests passed (100%). All frontend UI verifications passed. The OpenAI API key is properly masked in settings, table assignment works with/without OpenAI key, and Categories page is simplified (no AI button or clash group inputs).
passed_tests: GET /api/admin/settings returns masked openai_api_key (***xxxx format) - PASSED
Masked key (***xxxx) does NOT overwrite stored key when saving settings - PASSED
Table assignment completes (status=completed) even when no OpenAI key is set - PASSED
Table assignment completes with graceful AI skip when invalid OpenAI key is set - PASSED
GET /api/admin/categories returns categories with name and subcategory_count - PASSED
POST /api/admin/categories works without clash_group field - PASSED
Settings page shows 'OpenAI (AI Clash Detection)' section - PASSED (UI)
Settings page shows OpenAI API Key input with data-testid='settings-openai-key-input' - PASSED (UI)
Categories page has NO 'AI Clash Groups' button - PASSED (UI)
Categories page has NO inline clash group input fields - PASSED (UI)
Categories page shows category name and subcategory count cleanly (31 categories visible) - PASSED (UI)
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Used existing event and categories.
context_for_next_testing_agent: OpenAI API key integration tested. Key features verified: 1) OpenAI API key stored in site_settings with masking (***xxxx), 2) Masked keys don't overwrite actual stored keys, 3) Table assignment (_run_table_assignment) calls _ai_detect_clash_groups before seating algorithm, 4) AI detection gracefully skips if no key or invalid key, 5) Categories page simplified - no AI button or clash_group inputs. The seating.py _count_violations parameter was renamed from user_subcats to user_subclash (line 307).
